message:
fix(ft-dispatch): enforce dtype and device compatibility checks for binary and join operations

Previously, dispatch_tensor_binary_contiguous_f64 did not validate that
the two input tensors shared the same dtype and device before resolving
a dispatch key. This meant that a binary operation between e.g. an F64
CPU tensor and an F32 Cuda tensor would silently proceed through dispatch
key resolution and potentially produce incorrect results or panic deep
inside a kernel.

Similarly, dispatch_tensor_join_contiguous_f64 (used by cat/stack) only
inspected the first input's metadata when computing the dispatch keyset,
silently ignoring dtype or device mismatches in subsequent inputs.

Changes:
- Add ensure_tensor_meta_compatible() helper that returns a
  DispatchError::Kernel(KernelError::Incompatible(...)) when the lhs and
  rhs TensorMeta disagree on dtype or device, using the structured
  TensorCompatError variants for clear diagnostics.
- Call this helper at the top of dispatch_tensor_binary_contiguous_f64
  so binary ops fail closed before keyset resolution.
- Call this helper in a loop over all join inputs (comparing each against
  the first) so cat/stack fail closed on heterogeneous inputs.
- Import TensorCompatError from ft_core.
- Add tests: dispatch_join_rejects_dtype_mismatch and
  dispatch_join_rejects_device_mismatch to confirm structured error
  variants are returned.

This aligns the dispatch layer with the "fail closed" safety invariant:
operations on incompatible tensors are rejected at the dispatch boundary
rather than silently producing garbage or panicking later.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
